segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Certainly, in some cities bike-sharing has made a big contribution to that.
segments.zh: 当然，在一些城市，共享单车对此做出了很大贡献，也显著减少了路上的汽车数量。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: And also helped to cut the number of cars on the road significantly.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Which is the main point.
segments.zh: 这正是关键点。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。但我觉得它对噪音污染的影响没那么大，因为路上还是有很多公交车和卡车。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: But I'd say it's had less of an impact on noise pollution because there are still loads of buses and lorries around.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Shall we quickly discuss the recommendations we're going to make?
segments.zh: 我们快速讨论一下要提的建议吧？

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: In order to ensure bike-sharing schemes are successful?
segments.zh: 为了确保共享单车计划成功？

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。嗯，虽然我觉得拥有像GPS这样最先进的自行车很不错，但我不认为它们是绝对必要的。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Well, while I think it's nice to have really state-of-the art bikes with things like GPS, I wouldn't say they're absolutely necessary.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: But some technical things are really important – like a fully functional app – so people can make payments and book bikes easily.
segments.zh: 但有些技术性的东西真的很重要——比如功能完善的应用程序，这样人们就能轻松支付和预订自行车。那些在这方面没有投入的城市确实遇到了困难。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Places which haven't invested in that have really struggled.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Good point … Some people say there shouldn't be competing companies offering separate bike-sharing schemes, but in some really big cities, competition's beneficial and anyway one company might not be able to manage the whole thing.
segments.zh: 说得好……有人说不应该有相互竞争的公司提供各自的共享单车计划，但在一些非常大的城市，竞争是有益的，而且一家公司可能无法管理整个系统。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错。决定投资多少是个大问题。那些在推出共享单车计划的同时大量修建新自行车道的城市通常更成功——但也有成功的例子并非如此……不过真正重要的是大规模的宣传活动。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Deciding how much to invest is a big question.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Cities which have opened loads of new bike lanes at the same time as introducing bike-sharing schemes have generally been more successful – but there are examples of successful schemes where this hasn't happened … What does matter though – is having a big publicity campaign.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Definitely.
segments.zh: 绝对。如果人们不知道如何使用这个计划或者不了解它的好处，他们就不会使用。需要大力劝说人们放弃使用汽车。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: If people don't know how to use the scheme or don't understand its benefits, they won't use it.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: People need a lot of persuasion to stop using their cars.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Shall we look at some examples now?
segments.zh: 我们现在看一些例子好吗？说说我们认为它们好在哪里或不好在哪里。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: And say what we think is good or bad about them.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: I suppose we should start with Amsterdam as this was one of the first cities to have a bike-sharing scheme.
segments.zh: 我想我们应该从阿姆斯特丹开始，因为这是最早有共享单车计划的城市之一。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。那里本来就有浓厚的骑行文化。从某种意义上说，对共享单车的需求如此之大很奇怪，因为你可能会认为大多数人会用自己的自行车。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: There was already a strong culture of cycling here.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: In a way it's strange that there was such a demand for bike-sharing because you'd have thought most people would have used their own bikes.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: And yet it's one of the best-used schemes … Dublin's an interesting example of a success story.
segments.zh: 然而它却是使用率最高的计划之一……都柏林是一个成功案例的有趣例子。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: It must be because the public transport system's quite limited.
segments.zh: 肯定是因为公共交通系统太有限了。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Not really – there's no underground, but there are trams and a good bus network.
segments.zh: 也不完全是——没有地铁，但有有轨电车和不错的公交网络。我觉得价格是主要原因。这是欧洲最便宜的加入计划之一。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: I'd say price has a lot to do with it.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: It's one of the cheapest schemes in Europe to join.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: But the buses are really slow – anyway the weather certainly can't be a factor!
segments.zh: 但公交车真的很慢——反正天气肯定不是原因！

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: No – definitely not.
segments.zh: 对，绝对不是。伦敦的计划就挺成功的。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: The London scheme's been quite successful.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Yes – it's been a really good thing for the city.
segments.zh: 是的——这对城市来说真是件好事。自行车很受欢迎，整个系统维护得也很好，但扩张得不够快。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: The bikes are popular and the whole system is well maintained but it isn't expanding quickly enough.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Basically, not enough's been spent on increasing the number of cycle lanes.
segments.zh: 基本上，在增加自行车道方面投入不够。

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Hopefully that'll change.
segments.zh: 希望这种情况会改变。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。那欧洲以外的情况呢？

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Now what about outside Europe?

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: Well bike-sharing schemes have taken off in places like Buenos Aires.
segments.zh: 嗯，共享单车计划在布宜诺斯艾利斯这样的地方已经流行起来了。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。他们修建了庞大的自行车道网络来支持该计划的推行，对吧？这吸引了大量骑行者，而之前几乎没什么人骑车。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: They built a huge network of cycle lanes to support the introduction of the scheme there, didn't they?

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: It attracted huge numbers of cyclists where previously there were hardly any.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: An example of good planning.
segments.zh: 一个规划得当的例子。

items.prompt: Which TWO benefits of city bike-sharing schemes do the students agree are the most important?

pairs.note: benefits 被替换为 contribution，表示好处/贡献。
most important 被替换为 big，强调重要性。
原文直接出现 bike-sharing，与题干对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 bike-sharing 带来了 big contribution 并减少了汽车数量，直接对应题干中的 benefits 和 most important。

items.prompt: Which TWO benefits of city bike-sharing schemes do the students agree are the most important?

pairs.note: benefits 和 contribution 都表示好处、贡献。
most important 对应 big，强调重要性。
题干未直接出现，但答案对应减少汽车数量。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 bike-sharing 对减少汽车数量有重大贡献，这与题干中最重要的好处之一对应。

items.prompt: Which TWO things do the students think are necessary for successful bike-sharing schemes?

pairs.note: 题干问'necessary'，原文用'What does matter'表达必要条件，是替换。
原文直接重复'successful schemes'，但省略了'bike-sharing'，是部分重复。
题干问'things'，原文具体列出'publicity campaign'，是具体化替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句最后一句'What does matter though – is having a big publicity campaign'直接对应题干问的'necessary things'，其中'What does matter'替换'necessary'，'publicity campaign'是具体的一项。

items.prompt: Which TWO things do the students think are necessary for successful bike-sharing schemes?

pairs.note: 题干中的'successful bike-sharing schemes'在原文中简化为'successful schemes'，意思相同。
题干中的'big publicity campaign'与原文完全一致，直接对应。

pairs.q: opened loads of new bike lanes
pairs.p: opened loads of new bike lanes
pairs.note: 题干中的'opened loads of new bike lanes'与原文完全一致，但注意题干问的是'necessary'，而原文说'generally been more successful'，并非绝对必要，需结合上下文判断。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'What does matter though – is having a big publicity campaign'，直接对应题干中的'big publicity campaign'；同时前文提到'opened loads of new bike lanes'也是成功因素之一，但注意题干问的是'necessary'，而原文说'generally been more successful'，需注意区分。

pairs.note: 题干 Amsterdam 对应原文中的 here，指代地点。

paraphrase.explain: 题干 Amsterdam 在原文中被替换为 here，通过上下文可知 here 指代 Amsterdam。
